skill.name: vibe-kanban
skill.description: Manage AI coding agents on a Kanban board with git worktree isolation, MCP setup, health checks, cleanup scripts, and PR-oriented review workflows.
skill.summary: Coordinate parallel AI coding agents through a Kanban workflow with isolated git worktrees.
skill.author: supercent-io

security_audit.summary: Static analysis found many command, network, filesystem, and environment-variable patterns. Most Markdown command examples are benign documentation, but the skill includes runnable scripts and a Docker template that can modify local AI-tool configuration, source secret-bearing environment files, run agent CLIs, and mount the Docker socket. No prompt injection or confirmed malicious intent was found, so this is high risk rather than blocked.

high_findings.title: Docker Socket Mounted Into Container
high_findings.description: The Docker Compose template mounts the host Docker socket into the Vibe Kanban container. A compromised container or agent task could control host containers and access host resources.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: The mount is explicit and uses the host Docker socket path. This is a well-known container escape and host-control risk.

high_findings.title: Remote Agent Board With Secret-Bearing Configuration
high_findings.description: The skill supports remote board access and agent execution while documenting API keys and GitHub tokens. Misconfiguration could expose agent controls or credentials to a networked service.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines show remote mode and secret-bearing variables used by agent workflows. The risk depends on deployment controls, so this is high confidence but not malicious.

medium_findings.title: Scripts Modify User AI Tool Configuration
medium_findings.description: The MCP setup script creates or edits Claude and Codex configuration under the user home directory. This is expected for MCP setup but changes trusted tool behavior.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The script directly writes tool configuration files in home directories. The content is transparent and local, so this is a configuration-integrity risk.

medium_findings.title: Environment File Sourcing Can Load Secrets Into Agent Runtime
medium_findings.description: The start script sources a local .env file and exports values before running Vibe Kanban. This can expose API keys to agent processes and plugins.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The script intentionally loads .env into the process environment. This is common but sensitive because agent runtimes can inherit those variables.

medium_findings.title: Force Cleanup Can Delete Worktrees And Branches
medium_findings.description: The cleanup script can force-remove matching worktrees and branches. The dry-run option helps, but accidental broad cleanup can remove local work.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The destructive git commands are visible and scoped to matching names. The risk is operational data loss rather than hidden malicious behavior.

low_findings.title: Documentation Command Examples Trigger Static Command Alerts
low_findings.description: Many command-execution alerts are Markdown examples for installing, starting, and troubleshooting Vibe Kanban. These are user-invoked examples, not hidden execution.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited content is documentation or compressed reference text. It does not execute unless the user manually runs it.

low_findings.title: Localhost And Loopback URLs Are Expected Defaults
low_findings.description: Hardcoded localhost URLs and 127.0.0.1 addresses are used for local server and MCP defaults. They do not show external exfiltration by themselves.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The addresses point to local services and are consistent with the skill purpose. No evidence found that these endpoints transmit secrets externally.

dangerous_patterns.title: Privileged Container Host Access
dangerous_patterns.description: Mounting /var/run/docker.sock gives the container a powerful control channel to the host Docker daemon.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The exact socket mount is present. This pattern is dangerous even when intended for local development.

dangerous_patterns.title: Agent Runtime Receives Secret Environment Variables
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill documents API keys and token variables and loads .env before launching the service. Agent jobs may inherit sensitive values.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The evidence shows both secret-bearing variables and runtime loading. No direct exfiltration was found.

dangerous_patterns.title: Trusted AI Tool Configuration Mutation
dangerous_patterns.description: The setup script appends MCP server entries to Codex and writes Claude configuration files. Users should review these changes before use.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The writes are explicit and target trusted AI tool configuration. The server command is visible, so this needs warning rather than blocking.

content.user_title: Coordinate AI coding agents with Kanban
content.value_statement: Parallel AI coding work is hard to track across branches, logs, and pull requests. This skill provides a Kanban workflow for assigning agents, isolating worktrees, reviewing diffs, and preparing PRs.
actual_capabilities: Explains how to start Vibe Kanban with npx or the included start script.
Documents a workspace flow from task creation to agent execution and review.
Provides MCP setup examples for Claude Code, Codex, OpenCode, and related tools.
Includes helper scripts for health checks, MCP configuration, and worktree cleanup.
Describes git worktree isolation, branch naming, logs, diffs, and PR review steps.
Provides Docker and reverse proxy templates for remote deployment experiments.
limitations: It depends on external agent CLIs and their local authentication state.
Remote deployment requires careful network, CORS, and credential controls.
Cleanup scripts can remove local worktrees and branches if used broadly.
The Docker template is high risk because it mounts the host Docker socket.

use_cases.title: Coordinate parallel feature work
use_cases.description: Split an epic into independent cards and assign each card to a different AI coding agent.

use_cases.title: Review agent output before merge
use_cases.description: Use the board to inspect logs, diffs, and workspace state before creating or approving a pull request.

use_cases.title: Connect agents through MCP
use_cases.description: Configure Claude Code, Codex, or OpenCode to manage Vibe Kanban cards through MCP commands.

prompt_templates.title: Start a local board
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the vibe-kanban skill to start a local board on an open port. Check prerequisites and explain any missing agent authentication.

prompt_templates.title: Plan parallel cards
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the vibe-kanban workflow to split this feature into small independent cards. Recommend which agent should handle each card and why.

prompt_templates.title: Review a finished workspace
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the vibe-kanban review process to inspect the completed workspace. Summarize logs, diffs, risks, and PR readiness.

prompt_templates.title: Configure MCP safely
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the vibe-kanban MCP guidance to configure Codex and Claude Code. Show the config changes and identify any security warnings first.

output_examples.input: Create a board plan for a payment flow upgrade.
output: A set of small cards for frontend, backend, integration tests, and documentation.
Recommended agent assignments for each card.
Review checkpoints for logs, diffs, and pull request readiness.

output_examples.input: Check whether my Vibe Kanban server is ready.
output: A short status report covering server reachability, card counts, worktree state, and MCP availability.
Clear next steps if the server, MCP endpoint, or required CLI is missing.

output_examples.input: Prepare safe MCP setup for Codex.
output: A review of the target Codex config location.
The expected MCP server command and local port.
Warnings about trusted configuration changes and environment variables.

best_practices: Keep each card small enough for one focused branch and one reviewable pull request.
Review MCP configuration and environment variables before running setup scripts.
Use remote mode only on trusted networks with explicit allowed origins and limited credentials.
anti_patterns: Do not mount the Docker socket in shared or untrusted deployments.
Do not put broad cloud, GitHub, or model-provider tokens in a shared .env file.
Do not run cleanup with broad options before checking unmerged worktrees and branches.

faq.question: What does this skill help me do?
faq.answer: It helps coordinate AI coding agents on a Kanban board with isolated worktrees, logs, diffs, and PR-oriented review steps.

faq.question: Which tools does it support?
faq.answer: The skill documents workflows for Claude, Codex, Claude Code, Gemini, OpenCode, and other agent CLIs supported by Vibe Kanban.

faq.question: Does it run agents automatically?
faq.answer: The Vibe Kanban workflow can start a selected agent when a workspace or card enters the active execution flow.

faq.question: Why is the marketplace risk high?
faq.answer: The package includes scripts, secret-bearing environment variables, remote deployment guidance, config writes, and a Docker socket mount.

faq.question: Can I use it safely for local experiments?
faq.answer: Yes, but review scripts first, keep credentials narrow, avoid Docker socket mounts, and use local-only network bindings.

faq.question: Does the audit find malicious prompt injection?
faq.answer: No evidence found for prompt injection or confirmed malicious intent in the reviewed skill files.
